title: Digital Banking Operation Manager
description: Role Summary The Digital Banking Operations Manager is responsible for overseeing the daily operational activities and ensuring the smooth functioning of key units within the Digital Banking Department. This includes Card Issuance and Management, Settlement, Refund & Dispute Resolution, Customer Service and Support, and Business & Operations. The role ensures efficient service delivery, operational excellence, compliance with regulatory requirements, and enhanced customer experience across all digital banking services. Duties and Responsibilities Card Issuance and Management: Supervise the end-to-end card issuance process including debit, credit, prepaid and instant AfPay, MasterCards & VISA. Coordinate with vendors and internal stakeholders for timely card production, delivery, and stock management. Ensure compliance with card security standards and central bank regulations. Settlement, Refund & Dispute Resolution: Oversee daily transaction settlement and reconciliation processes with networks such as MasterCard, Visa, and AfPay. Manage refund processing and timely resolution of customer disputes and chargebacks. Coordinate with Finance, IT, and external partners to ensure settlement accuracy and integrity. Customer Service and Support: Lead the digital banking support team to provide timely and effective responses to customer inquiries and issues. Monitor service levels and continuously improve customer support processes. Handle escalated cases and ensure a professional and solution-oriented response. Coordinate with the marketing to effectively and efficiently advertise the digital banking products and services to attract customers and share accurate information about the AUB digital banking products and services in social medias and website. Leads the call center team for timely support for the digital banking services and products. Business and Operations: Coordinate daily business operations of the Digital Banking Department. Implement and monitor SOPs for all operational activities. Liaise with internal departments (IT, Risk, Compliance, Finance, etc.) for issue resolution and service enhancements. Track KPIs and generate performance reports for senior management. Lead accruals booking and manage digital banking operation cost and expenses. Cross-functional Responsibilities: Work closely with the Digital Banking Technical Manager and Deputy Head of DBD to align operations with technical developments and strategic initiatives. Support the rollout of new digital products and services. Ensure readiness for audits, regulatory reviews, and compliance reporting. Reporting and Documentation: Maintain detailed technical documentation, architecture designs, and process flows for all managed systems. Provide regular performance, risk, and incident reports to the Deputy Head and Head of DBD. Job Requirements Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Business Administration, Finance or related field (Master’s preferred). Experience: Minimum of 5 years of experience in digital banking systems, with at least 2 years in a technical leadership role. Technical Competencies: Strong understanding of digital banking services, card operations, and payment systems. Experience in settlement and dispute management. Excellent problem-solving, leadership, and communication skills. Familiarity with core banking systems and digital platforms. Fluency in English and local languages (Dari/Pashto) is an advantage. Knowledge of Core Banking Systems (CBS). Other Skills: Strong leadership and problem-solving abilities. Excellent communication and coordination skills. Fluency in English; knowledge of Dari and Pashto is an advantage. Submission Guidelines Interested candidates who deem themselves suitable for this vacancy can share their complete profile including (application letter, updated CV, and recent photo) electronically to below mentioned email and please indicate the vacancy number AUB/EA/VA/026-018 in the subject line of your email otherwise your application will not be considered.

title: Digital Marketing Manager
description:
About ZOODPAY
ZoodPay is a telecom services company that began its operations in 2023, founded by a dedicated and creative Afghan investor. The company aims to serve various business models, including B2B, B2C, and C2C, by providing a comprehensive prepaid electronic mobile top-up distribution service across the country.
The Zoodpay platform is developed and managed by a team of experienced professionals with expertise in telecom products, services distribution, and the fintech industry. Its advanced features are thoughtfully designed to meet the needs of merchants, offering a flexible and trustworthy platform that strategically secures their investments and ensures a healthy return on investment over time.
VISION:
Zoodpay is committed to innovation, creativity, and security. The platform offers multiple access channels, delivering the most secure and instantaneous prepaid mobile recharge sales and bundle activation services for mobile network operators (MNOs) customers, both within the country and abroad.
MISSION STATEMENT:
Zoodpay aims to become the leading on-demand electronic mobile top-up distribution platform for merchants and customers globally. Our platform is developed based on the feedback from merchants and customers, enabling all target users to benefit from a comprehensive mobile top-up distribution system that emphasizes ongoing development and collaboration.

Job Description
The Digital Marketing Officer will oversee a wide range of content creation and strategic communication tasks. This role involves developing and managing a content calendar that aligns with Zoodpay's program priorities, including creating engaging social media posts, videos, and graphics.
The officer will lead the production of digital campaigns for key events and manage the internal newsletter. Additionally, they will implement social media strategies to increase engagement and followers, analyze digital trends, and refine strategies based on data insights.
The incumbent will also spearhead digital marketing campaigns, collaborate with influencers, and ensure that all digital content adheres to Zoodpay's branding guidelines and strategic goals while maintaining brand consistency across all digital platforms.
Duties and responsibilities
Website Management: Oversee our Content Management System (CMS) by optimizing Search Engine Optimization (SEO). Ensure that all content is current, maintains a consistent tone of voice, provides a clear user journey, and measures performance against specific KPIs .
Content Creation: Write engaging content tailored for various audiences that aligns with brand guidelines. Stay informed about all aspects of Trees for Life’s work to ensure relevance and accuracy.
Social Media: Plan and coordinate a schedule of activities across platforms such as Facebook, Twitter, and YouTube to expand our social media audience .
Advertising: Manage Pay-Per-Click (PPC) advertising and online ad campaigns, regularly reviewing and adjusting keywords and ad groups to optimize performance. Additionally, maintain our presence on third-party sites, ensuring all messaging is current .
Email Marketing: Plan and execute a regular schedule of email marketing activities aimed at growing our subscriber base. Create engaging and relevant content, including testing strategies to optimize results.
Print Production: Coordinate the printing and production of our magazine, leaflets, banners, and other materials, including writing, editing, and proofing. Organize occasional print advertisements.
Brand Compliance: Implement brand guidelines and ensure adherence across all areas of work.
Reporting and Analysis: Monitor, report, and analyze results to enhance performance using tools like Google Analytics. Make necessary amendments and improvements based on findings.
Continuous Improvement: Stay updated on the latest trends and best practices in online marketing and measurement. Carry out additional duties as required, in line with the responsibilities of this position.
Motion Graphics Design : create visually engaging animations and videos that combine graphic design with animation to effectively communicate ideas through dynamic visuals. Will work on various projects, including social media content, website animations, and video marketing materials.
Graphic Design : create visual concepts to effectively communicate ideas and messages to consumers. They are responsible for the overall layout and production design of various applications, such as websites, advertisements, brochures, and magazines. Key responsibilities include meeting with Zoodpay Sr. Management to discuss project requirements, developing design concepts, selecting appropriate media and design elements, preparing drafts, and ensuring that the final product meets Zoodpay's needs and quality standards

Job Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Advertising, Communications, Business Administration, Digital Marketing, or a related field.
Proven 5+ years of working experience in digital marketing or a related role
Certification in Graphic Design and Digital Marketing is preferred.
Strong professional skills in graphic design, including image and video creation, with a high priority placed on motion graphics expertise.
Strong understanding of current online marketing concepts, strategies, and best practices.
Experience with SEO, PPC, Google Analytics, and various social media platforms.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to analyze data and provide evidence-based recommendations.
Creative thinking and problem-solving abilities.
Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
Familiarity with content management systems (CMS).
Strong attention to detail and organizational skills.

Submission Guidelines
Interested and qualified candidates are encouraged to submit their CVs along with a cover letter to hr@zood-pay.com by April 30, 2026. Please include the vacancy number, position title (VA-ZP-26-03 Digital Marketing Manager) in the subject line of your email; otherwise, your application will not be considered.

title: Graphic Designer – Agro-Climate Crop Handbooks
description:
About Researches Organization for Development (ROD)
The Research Organization for Development (ROD) is a registered Afghan non-governmental organization specializing in remote sensing, Geographic Information Systems (GIS), climate analysis, hydrology, and agricultural development. ROD operates through partnerships with leading international organizations including Welthungerhilfe (WHH), Catholic Relief Services (CRS), the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), the World Meteorological Organization (WMO), and the World Bank, drawing on a global network of more than one hundred researchers.
Under its ongoing partnership with Welthungerhilfe (WHH), ROD is implementing the Farmer Agro-Climate Crop Handbooks project across five provinces in Afghanistan's Northern Agro-Ecological Region. The project aims to translate complex climate, hydrological, and agronomic analysis into clear, visual, and practical guidance that supports smallholder farmers many of whom have limited literacy in making informed cropping decisions under changing climate conditions.
Each of the five target provinces will receive a dedicated, province-specific handbook reflecting local climate conditions, water resources, crop calendars, and twelve recommended crops. To ensure these handbooks are accessible, culturally appropriate, and visually compelling, ROD is seeking a qualified Graphic Designer to lead the visual development and production of all handbook materials and related communication products.

Job Description
The Graphic Designer will be responsible for transforming ROD's technical content into visually engaging, farmer-friendly publications that communicate climate-smart agricultural guidance effectively to low-literacy audiences in Dari and Pashto. The designer will work closely with ROD's technical team (GIS specialists, agronomists, hydrologists, and climate analysts) to develop a unified visual identity, a master template, and final print-ready handbooks and supporting materials.
3. Scope of Work
Under the direct supervision of the Project Lead / Principal Investigator, the Graphic Designer will undertake the following tasks:
3.1 Design Strategy and Visual Identity
Develop a coherent visual identity and style guide for the Agro-Climate Crop Handbook series, aligned with ROD's brand (teal #217178 and orange #F78F1E) and WHH's visual standards.
Propose and agree with the project team on a design approach suitable for low-literacy rural audiences, emphasizing clarity, iconography, and illustration over dense text.
Design a master layout template (Dari/Pashto RTL-compliant) that can be adapted across the five province-specific handbooks.
3.2 Handbook Layout and Production
Design and lay out five (5) province-specific Agro-Climate Crop Handbooks (one per province), each covering twelve recommended crops, climate profiles, water resources, crop calendars, risk advisories, and good agricultural practices.
Integrate GIS-derived maps, remote sensing outputs, and charts provided by the technical team into the handbook layouts, ensuring cartographic clarity and consistent styling.
Develop custom infographics and data visualizations for climate trends, crop calendars, water availability, and climate risk indicators.
Create a custom icon set and illustration library (hand-drawn or vector illustration style) tailored to Afghan rural contexts and culturally appropriate.
Ensure all typography, layout direction, and text flow fully comply with Dari and Pashto right-to-left (RTL) conventions and use appropriate Persian/Pashto fonts.
3.3 Supporting Communication Materials
Design farmer-facing posters summarizing key messages from each provincial handbook for display at community and extension level.
Design extension flyers and leaflets to complement the handbooks for field distribution by WHH and partners.
Design WHH-facing report covers, title pages, and layout templates for associated project reports and deliverables.
3.4 Review, Validation, and Finalization
Incorporate feedback from ROD's internal technical review, WHH review, and farmer validation focus group discussions conducted in the field.
Prepare final print-ready files (high-resolution PDF/X, CMYK color profile), packaged InDesign source files with all linked assets, editable vector files, and embedded/outlined fonts.
Provide digital versions (web-optimized PDFs) suitable for online publication and sharing.
3.5 Coordination and Documentation
Participate in regular coordination meetings with the Project Lead and technical team.
Maintain clear version control of all design files and submit editable source files at the end of the assignment.
Document the design style guide and template specifications to enable future updates by ROD.

Job Requirements
4. 1 Education
Bachelor's degree in Graphic Design, Visual Communication, Fine Arts, or a related field. A Master's degree or specialized certification is an asset.
4.2 Professional Experience
Minimum two (2) years of demonstrated professional experience for male candidate and one (1) year for female candidate in graphic design and editorial/publication layout, preferably for NGOs, international organizations, or development projects.
Proven track record designing publications for low-literacy or rural audiences, with a strong portfolio of farmer-facing, community-oriented, or extension materials.
Prior experience designing multilingual publications in Dari and/or Pashto (RTL layout) is essential.
Experience integrating maps, infographics, and data visualizations into printed materials is highly desirable.
4.3 Technical Skills (Essential)
Expert proficiency in Adobe InDesign and Adobe Illustrator (essential).
Strong skills in Adobe Photoshop and related graphic production tools.
Demonstrated ability to design infographics and data visualizations (climate data, crop calendars, risk charts).
Demonstrated experience with Dari/Pashto RTL typography, including appropriate Persian/Pashto font selection and layout conventions.
Strong illustration skills, preferably with a hand-drawn or custom vector style suitable for low-literacy audiences.
Sound understanding of print production workflows, color management (CMYK), and preparing press-ready files.
4.4 Desirable Skills
Familiarity with cartographic design principles and integrating GIS outputs into publications.
Experience designing for humanitarian or agricultural development projects in Afghanistan or similar contexts.
Basic knowledge of accessibility and inclusive design principles.
4.5 Language
Fluency in Dari and/or Pashto is required.
Working knowledge of English is required for communication with the technical team and reviewing source content.

Submission Guideline
Interested candidates are invited to submit the following documents:
A cover letter (maximum one page) outlining motivation and suitability for the position.
An updated Curriculum Vitae (CV) with at least two professional references.
A portfolio of previous relevant design work (PDF or web link), with particular emphasis on publications, infographics, and Dari/Pashto layouts.
Applications should be submitted by email to: hr@rod.af
Subject line: "Graphic Designer – Agro-Climate Crop Handbooks – [Applicant Name]"
Closing date: Open until filled. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, and interviews will be conducted as suitable candidates apply. The position will be closed once a desired candidate is identified and contracted.
Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Shortlisted applicants may be asked to complete a short design test as part of the selection process. ROD reserves the right to cancel, postpone, or modify this announcement at any stage.

Submission Email
hr@rod.af

title: Procurement officer
salary: As per company Salary Scale Policy
description: About Hindukush Bridge ICT-Services Company HKB-ICT is a leading internet and communication company aiming to make Afghanistan go online in ten coming years. Hindukush Bridge ICT Services Company is a Kabul-based company specializing in providing internet services to NOGs, Government, local offices, and Home Users in Afghanistan. This company is 100% Afghan-owned and a team of highly qualified professionals having over a decade of experience in the various areas of ISP and ICT sector with different national and international organizations manages its operations. Hindukush Bridge has a great partnership with most of the main players in the satellite and telecom industry. We are offering many added-value services to our partner/customer all over Afghanistan, along with a 24/7 hours Network Operation Center. Hindukush Bridge ICT Services Company functioning under a valid License Number (66724) issued by the Ministry of Commerce and Industry by the name of Hindukush Bridge ICT Services Company the Company has professional and technical staff in various fields such as surveying, analyzing, budget estimation, project management, reporting and documentation, data-related issues. Job Description A Procurement Officer is responsible for sourcing, negotiating, and managing contracts for telecom equipment, IT hardware/software, and services essential to network operations. The role requires strong vendor management, cost optimization, and compliance with industry standards. The Procurement Officer ensures timely and cost-effective acquisition of goods and services critical to ISP operations, including networking equipment, fiber optic cables, routers, servers, software licenses, and facility services. They act as the link between technical teams and suppliers to maintain uninterrupted service delivery. Key Responsibilities Supplier Management Identify, evaluate, and onboard reliable vendors for telecom and IT products. Negotiate contracts, pricing, and service-level agreements (SLAs). Maintain long-term supplier relationships to ensure continuity of supply. Procurement Operations Manage purchase requisitions and approvals in line with company policy. Ensure timely delivery of equipment and services to support network expansion and maintenance. Monitor inventory levels and coordinate with warehouse/logistics teams. Cost & Compliance Optimize procurement costs while maintaining quality standards. Ensure compliance with local regulations, ISP licensing requirements, and company procurement policies. Prepare procurement reports for management and auditors. Cross-Functional Collaboration Work closely with engineering, IT, and finance departments to align procurement with technical needs and budget. Support project teams in sourcing specialized equipment for new installations or upgrades. title: RAN Drive Test Engineer
description: Role Summary The DT Engineer is responsible for performing network drive test (DT) activities on the Radio Access Network (RAN) across multi-vendor and multi-technology 2G, 3G, and LTE sites. Duties and Responsibilities Network DT activities performed on the RAN covering multi-vendor/multi-technology 2G, 3G & LTE sites. Detailed knowledge of all OEM parameter and feature sets as well as recommended settings, OEM KPI’s and counters, counters specific to measure subscriber experience, Handling Post Processing procedures/ guidelines. Identification of KPIs relating to coverage, accessibility, mobility, integrity, and service retainability for optimization for all Radio bearers/ services including Voice and Data. Proactively identify and escalate areas with network coverage issues, poor network services, loss of signal strength and data service faults on the network. Customer Complaint management which includes both proactive and reactive quality improvement initiatives. Coordinating with Optimization teams, scheduling daily, weekly, monthly, Ad-hoc activities to perform detailed DTs. RF Coverage analysis, parameter optimizations, antenna tilt verifications and suggestions. Periodic network motorway drive test campaigns in major metros, Ad-hoc drive testing for quality benchmarking and RAN Optimisation activities including Single Site Verification Test report for newly integrated sites of technologies. Maintaining a parameter & customer complaint database which details all the RAN parameters under control of the Optimisation Dept. Analyzed logs of Radio tools for identifying issues in the coverage and quality area. Proactively identify and escalate areas with network coverage issues and faults in the network. Collaborate with business support service teams to identify new potential sales areas. Must have Hands-on Routine network activities. Radio Network Topology Audit, Radio Network Parameter consistency checks Major route drive testing, Malls, IBS/DAS, walk/drive testing. Sign-off the performance acceptance as per agreed Quality Targets for all modernization projects. Strong Hands-on and Knowledge of DT & Post Processing Tools (e.g., TEMS, Nemo, MapInfo, Actix, GE Tools). OH&S/QMS Role and Responsibilities: Reports any hazards or risks in addition to Accidents/Incident to QHSE department. Be aware of and complies to i engineering’s IMS Policy. Abides by i engineering’s Local Legal and Client Requirements. Attends and engages in IMS Awareness Sessions. Ensures that all IMS procedures are regularly followed and raises the issue when they are not Job Requirements Minimum 2-3 years of experience. Must have strong telecom skills and knowledge of common programs such as Microsoft Office. Ability to work under pressure. Capable of professionally managing confidential information. title: Country IT & Business Solutions Officer
description: Role Summary The IT & Business Solutions Officer (Officer) is responsible for overseeing and managing all aspects of IT operations in a country where Women for Women International operates. This role requires a strong technical background, excellent communication skills, and the ability to work independently while collaborating with cross-functional teams. The Officer will ensure the smooth functioning of all IT systems, infrastructure, and services in the country, aligning them with the overall Global IT strategy and organizational objectives. Additionally, the Officer will play a crucial role in improving business processes, increasing efficiencies, providing regular applications and information security training, successfully implementing initiatives directed from the Global IT team, leading both local and global projects, contributing to the advancement of Global IT's annual plans, and ensuring compliance with GDPR regulations and any other applicable laws in the country. Duties and Responsibilities Engagement Internal Relations Collaborate closely with the Admin Coordinator and Global IT team to align country-level IT operations with organizational strategies. Serve as the primary IT focal point for all departments within the Afghanistan Country Office, ensuring smooth communication and coordination. Work cross-functionally with Program, Finance, Administration, and Operations teams to identify business process improvements and technology-driven efficiencies. Provide IT advice and guidance to senior leadership and staff, fostering a culture of digital literacy and security awareness. External Relations Manage relationships with local IT vendors, service providers, and consultants, ensuring compliance with organizational standards and service-level agreements. Liaise with internet service providers, equipment suppliers, and external technical support as required. Delivery IT Infrastructure Management : Manage and maintain the IT infrastructure in the country, including network devices, storage systems, and telecommunications equipment. Ensure that the infrastructure is secure, scalable, and optimized for efficient operations. Systems Administration : Administer and support the country office's computer systems, including operating systems, software applications, and user accounts following the standards and procedures set by Global IT. Monitor system performance, troubleshoot issues, and implement necessary upgrades or patches. Technical/Helpdesk Support : Provide technical support and assistance to end-users in the country, addressing hardware and software problems, network connectivity issues, and other IT-related queries. Resolve support requests in a timely manner to minimize downtime and disruptions and escalate requests to Global IT promptly when needed. Utilize Global IT's ticketing system to track and manage support requests, ensuring efficient communication and documentation. Network and Connectivity : Manage the local network infrastructure, including routers, switches, firewalls, and wireless access points. Ensure reliable connectivity within the office and to the Internet, implementing necessary security measures to protect data and systems. IT Security and Training : Implement and enforce IT security policies and procedures in accordance with the organization's standards. Regularly assess and monitor the country office's IT security posture, identifying vulnerabilities and recommending appropriate measures to mitigate risks. Conduct regular applications and information security training for all users, with a specific focus on data protection and maximizing the use of available systems. IT Procurement and Vendor Management: Collaborate with the Global IT team and local stakeholders to identify IT needs and procure necessary hardware, software, and services for the country. Manage vendor relationships and contracts, ensuring compliance with service level agreements. Business Process Improvement: Work closely with other teams in the country office to identify areas for process improvement and increased efficiency. Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business requirements and provide IT solutions that streamline operations. Act as a consultant and advisor on IT-related matters, helping teams successfully implement new initiatives directed by the Global IT team. Lead both local and global projects and contribute to the advancement of Global IT's annual plans. Backup and Disaster Recovery: Ensure information across devices and systems is properly backed up. Develop and maintain a country’s disaster recovery plan to ensure business continuity in various crisis scenarios. Regularly test and validate these plans' effectiveness, making necessary improvements to ensure business continuity in case of disruptions or outages. Documentation and Reporting: Create and maintain comprehensive documentation of IT systems and assets, processes, and configurations in the country office. Prepare regular reports on IT operations, including performance metrics, incident trends, and project updates for monthly management review. Utilize Global IT's knowledge management system to document changes and create help materials for users. Compliance with GDPR and Applicable Laws : Ensure compliance with GDPR regulations and any other applicable data protection laws in the country. Implement measures to safeguard personal data and privacy rights. Conduct regular audits and assessments to verify compliance and provide reports to the appropriate stakeholders. Business Process Improvement: Work closely with other teams in the country office to identify areas for process improvement and increased efficiency. Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business requirements and provide IT solutions that streamline operations. Act as a consultant and advisor on IT-related matters, helping teams successfully implement new initiatives directed by the Global IT team. Lead both local and global projects and contribute to the advancement of Global IT's annual plans. Job Requirements Job Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field. Proven experience in IT operations, infrastructure management, and technical support (3+ years preferred). Strong knowledge of networking principles, systems administration, and IT security. Familiarity with relevant technologies, such as Windows/Mac, Android and iOS operating systems, virtualization, cloud services, and enterprise applications. Certifications in relevant areas (e.g., CCNA, Meraki, MCSA, ITIL) are a plus. Ability to work independently and collaboratively as part of a team. Fluent written and verbal Dari, Pashto language skills; please expect written fluency testing. Fluent written and verbal English language skills Willing and able to travel to sub-offices as needed as well as travel internationally. Demonstrated ability to take decisions proactively , work autonomously, and solicit team and leadership support when appropriate. Attention to details, Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, strong use of logic and creativity . Impeccable integrity. Demonstrated ability to navigate Afghanistan’s laws and regulations, and to effectively, respectfully , responsibly, and ethically manage contextual and extraordinary complexities. Meticulous organizational and time-management skills; able to prioritize and manage order of operations within multiple projects, directing and maximizing team inputs while: proactively supporting team wellbeing. Committed to progress in the workplace, including progress in equity for all , diversity, inclusion, and belonging. Working Conditions: This position typically operates in an office environment. Occasional overtime may be required to meet deadlines or complete projects. Submission Guidelines Please clearly mention the Job Title and Vacancy Number in the subject line of your email. We appreciate your interest in joining our team.

title: IT Officer NOC Engineer - Night Shift
description: Role Summary To serve as the frontline guardian of Setaragan Mutahed’s high-volume Airtime Top-Up infrastructure and StarPay EMI. This role ensures 24/7 availability of critical financial transactions and telecommunication links. You will monitor production systems, mitigate real-time payment failures, and ensure that overnight batch settlements and EMI reconciliations are processed without error, providing a seamless experience for thousands of agents and end-users. Duties and Responsibilities • Continuous Infrastructure & Transaction Monitoring: Proactively monitor core infrastructure, server health, and network links. Use approved tools to track the real-time flow of Top-Up transactions and StarPay EMI payments, identifying any latency or drops in success rates. • L1 Incident Triage & FinTech Troubleshooting: Perform immediate first-level triage on system alarms. For payment failures or "timeout" errors, collect critical evidence (API logs, transaction IDs, screenshots) and execute approved runbooks to restore service. • Critical Escalation Management: Act as the primary point of contact during the night shift. Escalate high-severity incidents (e.g., core database downtime, ISP link failure, or payment gateway outages) to on-call Tier-2 engineers or vendor support (MNOs/Banks) according to the strict Escalation Matrix. • Rigorous Ticketing & Shift Handover: Document every incident in the Jira/Ticketing system with high precision. Provide a comprehensive Morning Handover Report detailing pending issues, vendor tickets, and critical status updates to the daytime operations team. • Operational Health Checks: Conduct routine checks on server CPU/RAM, disk usage, and service statuses. Ensure all scheduled automated jobs and security backups are completed, reporting any exceptions immediately to prevent morning backlog. • Overnight Batch & EMI Reconciliation: Verify the successful execution of overnight batch reconciliation jobs between StarPay and partner banks. Manually trigger or report failures in settlement processes to ensure financial data integrity for the next business day. • Network & ISP Coordination: Support basic network troubleshooting (link down, packet loss) affecting agent connectivity. Coordinate directly with local ISPs and MNO partners (AWCC, Roshan, Etisalat, MTN, Salaam) to resolve connectivity issues affecting the Top-Up platform. • Compliance & Security Adherence: Maintain absolute confidentiality of sensitive financial and customer data. Follow strict Access Control Procedures and Zero-Change Policy—do not perform unauthorized system changes or configuration updates during the shift. Job Requirements • Education: Bachelor’s degree or Diploma in Computer Science, Information Technology, or Software Engineering. • Experience: 1–3 years of experience in a Network Operations Center (NOC), IT Support, or FinTech operations role. Fresh graduates with exceptional understanding of Linux/Networking and a high sense of responsibility will be considered. • Shift Flexibility: Absolute willingness to work permanent night shifts and remain alert during quiet hours to handle sudden, time-critical emergencies. Technical Skills • Networking Foundations: Strong grasp of TCP/IP, DNS, VPNs, and troubleshooting tools (Ping, MTR, Traceroute, Telnet). • Systems Knowledge: Familiarity with Windows/Linux environments, basic SQL queries (for transaction lookups), and monitoring dashboards (e.g., Zabbix, Grafana, or PRTG). • FinTech Literacy: Understanding of API response codes (200, 404, 500) and the basic flow of a digital payment transaction. • Documentation: High proficiency in professional English/Local language for writing technical incident logs and handover notes. Soft Skills & Behavioral Competencies • Crisis Management: Ability to remain calm and methodical when multiple systems trigger alarms simultaneously. • Sense of Ownership: A "pro-active" rather than "reactive" mindset; taking responsibility for a ticket until it is resolved or officially handed over. • Reliability: Strict punctuality and adherence to operational procedures, recognizing that StarPay’s reputation relies on 100% system uptime. • Communication: Clear, concise communication skills to explain technical issues to both engineers and non-technical stakeholders. Submission Guidelines Interested and qualified national candidates are invited to click the link below and fill in the form completely. submit their applications, including a cover letter and an updated CV , no later than 05 May, 2026 to the link provided below: Admission Link: ( https://forms.gle/jANFWERiLvYSTRwc7 ) Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an interview.

title: Specialist IT Security
description: Role Summary Specialist IT Security with Etisalat Afghanistan. Duties and Responsibilities • Monitor and analyze network traffic, system activities, and security events to detect potential intrusions, attacks, or vulnerabilities. • Respond to, investigate, and remediate security incidents, ensuring timely resolution and proper escalation where required. • Perform regular risk assessments, vulnerability assessments, and security audits to identify and mitigate potential risks. • Conduct penetration testing and security validation exercises to proactively identify weaknesses in systems, applications, and network infrastructure. • Prepare detailed incident reports, root cause analyses, and recommendations for continuous improvement. • Manage, maintain, and troubleshoot security infrastructure, including firewalls, VPN (SSL/IPsec), encryption systems, DMZ environments, and endpoint protection solutions. • Monitor endpoints using EDR and NDR solutions to detect, analyze, and respond to advanced threats. • Utilize SIEM & SOAR platforms for log monitoring, correlation, and threat detection. • Monitor and secure Active Directory and identity systems using relevant security tools. • Administer and support security solutions such as NAC, PAM, and IAM, including daily operations and troubleshooting. • Integrate network devices and servers (Linux/Windows) with SIEM and other centralized security platforms. • Collaborate with internal teams and attend technical sessions to ensure alignment of security operations and initiatives. • Support the implementation and follow-up of cybersecurity projects and initiatives across the organization. • Promote security awareness by guiding and training staff on cybersecurity best practices. • Stay updated with the latest cybersecurity threats, vulnerabilities, and technologies to enhance the organization’s security posture. • Perform any additional tasks assigned by management in line with organizational objectives. Job Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology or a related field, with at least four years of relevant experience preferred. For further information, please contact us via WhatsApp at +93 (0) 788 887 363.

title: Chief Information Officer
description: Role Summary The Chief Information Officer (CIO) is a senior executive responsible for leading the bank’s overall information technology (IT) strategy and operations. The CIO ensures that technology systems support the bank’s strategic goals, enhance operational efficiency, and comply with regulatory requirements. This role involves overseeing IT infrastructure, digital banking systems, cybersecurity frameworks, and data management practices. The CIO plays a critical role in driving digital transformation, improving customer experience through technology, and ensuring the security and integrity of all banking systems. The CIO collaborates with executive management and other departments to identify technological needs, implement innovative solutions, and manage IT risks. Additionally, the role ensures business continuity, system reliability, and alignment of IT initiatives with the bank’s long-term objectives. Duties and Responsibilities Chief Information Officer shall develop goals, roadmap and executing a multi-year technology strategy supporting the full range of business services, and ensure the IT department runs smoothly and effectively. Chief Information Officer shall provide vision and leadership for developing and implementing information technology initiatives and Chief Information Officer is a member of the Executive Leadership Team and directs the planning and implementation of enterprise IT systems in support of business operations in order to improve cost effectiveness and service quality, Chief Information Officer shall direct activities of the IT Department, providing leadership and supervising of IT staff and ensures Compliance with bank’s policies and procedures as well monitor changes in the technology sector to discover ways the company can improve and develop. Chief Information Officer is responsible for daily operations of technology and technology-related systems and in providing ongoing service to internal staff and other stakeholders, also responsible for all aspects of the organization’s information technology and systems, including information and cybersecurity. Chief Information Officer Shall Lead IT strategic and operational planning to achieve business goals by fostering innovation, prioritizing IT initiatives, and coordinating the evaluation, deployment and management of current and future IT resources and systems across the organization. Chief Information Officer Shall Create state of the organization dynamic security practices across the IT department and bank as well provide high quality day-to-day operations of technology and technology enabled business functions. Chief Information Officer shall develop and maintain an appropriate IT organizational structure that supports the organization’s needs, including information and cybersecurity. Chief Information Officer shall stay abreast of emerging technologies that can improve IT department performance and bring cost effectiveness to the company. Chief Information Officer responsible to stay at the forefront of IT risk and security issues facing the Bank also Assess and make recommendations on the improvement or re-engineering of the IT department. Chief Information Officer shall maintain security standards appropriate for the bank’s size and complexity and Develop, track, and control the IT annual operating and capital budgets. Chief Information Officer shall collaborate with senior leaders to ensure technology strategy and investment are in line with business function objectives and Acting as a senior point of escalation to troubleshoot, report and resolve issues as well supervise the networks and computer systems including Core Banking applications and Databases in the company to ensure optimal performance. Chief Information Officer shall serve as the senior technical solutions analyst and ensuring strategic technology decisions are evaluated and assessed in line with technology industry standards. Chief Information Officer shall identify security threats, formulate strategies and methods to mitigate the threats. Chief Information Officer Shall Plan, Direct and establish IT-related projects as well create and adapt technological platforms to improve the customer experience. Chief Information Officer is responsible for Troubleshoot date-related issues and establish regular maintenance. Chief Information Officer shall work with upper-level IT staff to develop and implement department goals and implementing IT policies, protocols and standards. Chief Information officer shall Develops and implement strategies to improve the efficiency and efficacy of technologies initiatives appropriate support to company-wide goals. Chief Information Officer is responsible to review performance of IT staff and develop their Job Descriptions. Chief Information Officer is Responsible for the development, review and certification of all back-up and disaster recovery procedures and plans as well planning data storage and monitoring performance of databases. Chief Information officer shall Train IT staff personnel to enhance their skills and knowledge. Chief Information officer shall monitor and regular check-up of IT succession plan and develop succession plan for key IT positions. Chief Information Officer shall work with upper management and senior IT staff to develop the IT department budget as well review and recommend IT department purchases and payments. Chief Information officer shall Review Vendor proposals and makes decisions based on things such as price, what they offer, and how the vendor product may fit into the organization. Chief Information officer shall communicate with other executives regarding costs, value and risk-potential of new IT projects. Chief Information Officer Shall Oversees the development and testing of new programs developed specifically for the company. Chief Information Officer Shall Ensures the company’s data remains secure by keeping up to date on the latest cyber security threats and finds ways to guard against them on an organization-wide scale. Chief Information officer shall Coaches members of the IT teams, particularly IT leaders, so everyone stays on the same pate across the company. Chief Information officer shall Communicates with other executive members to make sure all parts of the company use technology in the best ways possible. To attend to any other matter that may not have been mentioned in this document but is otherwise required to be attended by the job holder. Besides, the Job Holder is to attend to any Bank related assignment given by CEO, Board of Supervisors or General Meeting of Share Holders. Job Requirements Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science required; Master’s degree preferred. Professional Certificates in cybersecurity, information Technology, and modern threats. Experience: Minimum 7-10 Year experience in managing or directing IT operation in financial institutions. Skills: Proven experience as chief information officer or similar managerial role. Excellent knowledge of IT systems and infrastructure. Background in designing/developing IT systems and planning implementation. Solid understanding of data analysis, budgeting and business operations. Superior analytical and problem-solving capabilities. Strong strategic and business mindset. Excellent organizational and leadership skills. Outstanding communication and interpersonal abilities. Personal Attributes: Visionary thinker Perseverance Patience Risk Taking Ability Outgoing Personality Service-oriented skills Leadership Skills: Supervisory skills Motivational skills Ability to take ownership Delegation of work Entrepreneurial skills Staff Management Skills Decision-Making Skills Submission Guidelines Please mention specific Job Title and Vacancy numbers in subject line of your E-mail otherwise your application will not be considered. title: Commercial Reporting Officer
description: Role Summary The reporting officer is responsible for ensuring the accurate collection, validation, analysis, and reporting of all commercial data across key businesses, including Top-Up, Star Pay, and Shops. The role supports strategic decision-making, performance monitoring, and continuous improvement through reliable MIS systems and actionable insights. Duties and Responsibilities 1. Data Collection & Management • Lead the systematic collection of data from all commercial projects, regional teams, and operational channels. • Ensure data integrity by maintaining structured, reliable, and well-organized databases. • Implement data validation checks to ensure accuracy, completeness, and consistency across all reporting sources. 2. Reporting & Business Analysis • Prepare and deliver high-quality daily, weekly, and monthly reports for the Head of Commercial and senior management. • Monitor and report KPIs, sales performance, and regional performance metrics. • Conduct detailed data analysis to identify trends, performance gaps, and growth opportunities. • Provide clear, actionable insights to support strategic and operational decision-making. 3. Performance Monitoring & KPI Tracking • Track and monitor targets, and operational KPIs across Top-Up, Star Pay, and Shops projects. • Ensure real-time visibility of performance against defined business objectives. • Highlight underperformance or risk areas and escalate to relevant stakeholders for timely corrective action. 4. Cross-Functional Coordination & Support • Collaborate closely with product owners, main office, and regional teams to ensure timely and accurate data flow. • Provide standardized reporting formats, dashboards, and templates to support operations. 5. Process Improvement & Automation • Continuously evaluate existing reporting processes and identify inefficiencies or gaps. • Recommend and implement improvements to enhance reporting accuracy, speed, and usability. • Drive automation initiatives to reduce manual reporting efforts and improve data reliability. • Support system enhancements and digital transformation of MIS processes where applicable. 6. Data Governance & Documentation • Maintain proper documentation of reporting structures, data sources, and KPI definitions. • Ensure consistency in reporting standards across all business units. • Support audit requirements by ensuring data traceability and historical record management. Job Requirements Education • Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, Statistics, Data Science, or a related field • A Master’s degree or relevant certifications in Data Analysis, MIS, or Business Intelligence is an advantage Experience • Minimum 2–4 years of experience in reporting, MIS, data analysis, or a similar role • Experience in telecom, fintech, banking, or FMCG sectors is preferred • Proven experience in handling large datasets, reporting systems, and performance tracking Skills & Competencies • Strong analytical and critical thinking skills with the ability to interpret complex data • High attention to accuracy, detail, and data integrity • Ability to translate data into actionable insights for business decisions • Strong understanding of KPIs, performance tracking, and business metrics • Excellent problem-solving and process improvement skills • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, formulas, data analysis tools) • Experience with BI tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau) is highly preferred • Familiarity with databases, SQL, or data management systems is an advantage • Experience with ERP/CRM systems and reporting dashboards • Ability to design and maintain automated reports and dashboards Submission Guidelines Application Submission Guidelines Interested and qualified national candidates are invited to click the link below and fill in the form completely. submit their applications, including a cover letter and an updated CV , no later than 10 May, 2026 to the link provided below: Submission Form: ( https://forms.gle/WNnFXjsFKYVzEGdC6 ) Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an interview.

title: IT Manager
category: IT Billing, IT - Hardware, IT - Software, IT Support
description: Role Summary AurumPay is seeking an experienced and hands-on IT Manager to lead and manage our core technical infrastructure. As a licensed Electronic Money Institution (EMI), this role is critical in ensuring 99.99% uptime , security, and regulatory compliance of our digital wallet platform. The ideal candidate will have deep expertise in Oracle Solaris environments and Sophos security infrastructure , and will act as a bridge between development, security, and operations teams to ensure systems are secure, scalable, and audit-ready. Duties and Responsibilities Key Responsibilities 1. Server Infrastructure (Oracle Solaris & Enterprise Systems) Lead the installation, configuration, and hardening of Oracle Solaris servers Configure and manage Automated Installer (AI) for network-based provisioning Administer Oracle Solaris Zones (containers) to isolate application environments Monitor system performance, including kernel tuning and resource management (ZFS, DTrace) Implement backup strategies, including ZFS snapshots and disaster recovery procedures 2. Network Security (Sophos Firewall & Perimeter Defense) Deploy, configure, and maintain Sophos Firewall (XG/XGS series) Implement firewall policies, VLAN segmentation, and access control lists (ACLs) Manage secure connectivity through Site-to-Site (IPsec/SSL) and SSL VPN Monitor and optimize threat protection tools, including MDR, IPS, Web Filtering, and Application Control 3. EMI & Fintech Compliance · Ensure all configurations align with Central Bank (DAB) regulations for EMI operations. · Maintain logs for all access to financial systems; prepare for internal and external audits. · Lead IT audit and change management processes – no unapproved changes to firewall, Solaris zones, or payment integrations. · Manage Business Continuity (BCP) and Disaster Recovery (DR) drills with measurable targets: RTO < 1 hour, RPO < 15 minutes for transaction systems. 4. IT Operations & Management Manage Windows/Linux servers where the application stack interfaces with Solaris. Oversee vendor management for internet links and hardware procurement. Lead and support the internal IT helpdesk team 5. Budget & Vendor Management (Critical addition) · Prepare and manage the annual IT budget, including hardware, software licenses (Oracle, Sophos), and support contracts. · Negotiate with vendors for internet links, data center services, and hardware procurement. title: Sourcing & Quotation Analyst
category: Procurement and logistics, Information Technology, Business Development, Sales, Supply Chain Management
description: Role Summary We are hiring a Sourcing & Quotation Analyst to join our remote team. This is a night shift role. Your shift starts at 5:00 PM Kabul time and runs for 8 hours, aligned with international business hours. Your day will revolve around three things: finding the right opportunities, sourcing the right items from the right vendors, and building clean quotations that win. You will spend most of your shift on calls and emails with international vendors, so strong English and confidence on the phone are non-negotiable. This role is for someone serious. Someone who wants to grow, works hard without being chased, and takes pride in getting the details right. If you are looking for a relaxed 9 to 5, this is not for you. If you want to build a real career and you are willing to put in the work, we want to talk to you. Duties and Responsibilities Opportunity Research Search online portals and platforms for new tenders, RFQs, and product opportunities. Read the requirements carefully and understand exactly what is being asked for. Shortlist the opportunities that fit and flag them to management. Item Sourcing Identify the correct manufacturers and authorized distributors for each item. Verify part numbers, specifications, and compliance requirements before reaching out. Find backup vendors for every key item so we are never stuck with a single source. Vendor Communication Call and email vendors during your shift to request quotes, confirm stock, lead times, and shipping terms. Ask sharp follow-up questions when a quote is incomplete or unclear. Follow up consistently until you have everything you need. No half-finished work. Quotation Preparation Organize vendor pricing in Excel or Google Sheets and compare properly. Build professional quotations using company templates. Triple check every number, SKU, quantity, and date before anything goes out. Coordination and Records Keep clean records of all vendor communication, quotes, and follow-ups. Update internal trackers and CRM tools daily. Work closely with management on final pricing and submission strategy. Never miss a deadline. Discipline and Work Ethic Log in on time. Every shift. Respond quickly on email, WhatsApp, and internal tools during your hours. Keep a professional tone in every vendor interaction. Own your work. No excuses, no shortcuts. Job Requirements Must-Have Fluent English. Spoken and written. You must be able to hold a clear, confident conversation on the phone with international vendors. This is not negotiable. Strong IT background. Comfortable with advanced Google search, Excel or Google Sheets (formulas, filters, sorting), email tools, cloud storage, and basic CRM systems. Sharp critical thinking. You can read a requirement, understand what is being asked, and spot gaps or risks in a vendor's quote. Night shift availability. Ready to work 5:00 PM to 1:00 AM Kabul time, Monday to Friday, without exception. Serious attention to detail. Numbers, SKUs, quantities, and dates must always be right. Discipline and work ethic. Self-driven, reliable, consistent. You do not need someone standing over you to get the work done. Confident on calls. Polite, professional, clear. No slang, no shortcuts. Hungry. You want to build something real and you are willing to put in the work to get there. Nice-to-Have Previous experience in procurement, sourcing, or supply chain. Exposure to international tenders or B2B quotation work. Experience with tender portals or sourcing platforms. Familiarity with Notion, ClickUp, Trello, or similar project management tools. Basic knowledge of IT hardware, industrial equipment, or logistics. Do Not Apply If You are looking for a 9 to 5. You cannot commit to the night shift. You need to be chased to finish work. Your English is weak and you plan to fake it on vendor calls. You cannot. Submission Guidelines Interested candidates should submit their updated CV along with a short introduction (2 to 3 paragraphs) explaining why they are a good fit for this role and confirming their availability for the night shift (5:00 PM to 1:00 AM Kabul time). Send applications to: procurement@vastglobelogistics.com Please write "Sourcing & Quotation Analyst Application" in the email subject line. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Applications without a proper introduction will not be considered.

description: About Speenkai Speenkai is Afghanistan's first and leading BPO (Business Process Outsourcing) service company, specializing in providing comprehensive administrative and technical support to Afghan-owned companies abroad. Job Summary Night-Shift Job Are you a motivated, detail-oriented professional fluent in English, and ready to grow in a dynamic environment? Speenkai, Afghanistan's first BPO service company, and Bay Master Appliance Services LLC are looking to hire experienced individuals to join our full-time night shift team as Dispatchers/Customer Service Representatives.
